summary: Full e2e validation of Anemia Screening App. Backend: 12/12 pytest tests passed (health, auth register/login/me/duplicate/invalid, screenings create with Gemini vision, list-without-images, get-full, delete, fusion threshold matches risk_percent). Frontend: full UX flows verified including landing, register, login, protected redirects, screening upload + Gemini analysis + redirect to results page with all 3 RiskCards (eye/nail/fusion) + GradCAM sliders + findings + Download report button, history listing, FAQ on Learn page, logout. Gemini analysis worked end-to-end (returned valid JSON, fusion threshold respected).

ui_bugs.issue: After completing a new screening on /screen and navigating to /history (after having previously visited /history earlier in the session when it was empty), the list still shows 'No screenings yet'. The newly-created screening only appears after a full page reload. Looks like History.js does not refetch on mount/route-change (stale cache). Backend GET /api/screenings returns the record correctly (verified by backend tests).

Success toast ('Welcome back', 'Analysis complete') is rendered overlapping the center header nav links (New scan / History / Learn). On 1920px desktop the toast sits exactly on top of the nav buttons for ~3-4 seconds, blocking clicks during that window. Consider positioning toasts to top-right and below the header.

issues: Toast notification overlaps the centered nav menu on desktop (1920w). Should be offset below header or moved to a corner that doesn't intersect navigation.

action_items: Fix History.js to refetch screenings on mount / on route change (e.g., useEffect with location dep, or invalidate cache after POST /api/screenings).
Reposition success toasts so they don't sit over the header nav links (move to top-right corner, or below header).

Optional: Tighten CORS — currently allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False; cookie is set with samesite='none' secure=true, so cookies will still flow on same origin but cross-origin cookie auth would be blocked. App uses Bearer header so this is OK but worth noting.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 388: allow_credentials=False with allow_origins=['*'] is intentional but disables cookie-based auth for cross-origin clients. Since frontend uses Bearer token from localStorage this is fine, but the access_token cookie that is set will never be sent cross-origin. Either drop the cookie or set explicit origin + allow_credentials=True.
server.py: No brute-force lockout / rate limiting on /api/auth/login (auth_testing.md recommends 5-fail lockout). Acceptable for a demo prototype but worth flagging.

server.py compute_fusion uses confidence 0 with floor 0.1 — correct, but when one modality has confidence 0 (image unusable) it still contributes 0.1 weight. Consider zero-weight when image_type marked unusable.
Screenings store full base64 images in MongoDB documents. For larger images this could push docs past 16MB. Consider GridFS or object storage if you scale beyond demo.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@anemiacheck.app / Admin@123 (seed verified working). Random test users registered via /api/auth/register.
seed_data_creation: Backend pytest creates several TEST_-prefixed users via /api/auth/register and one screening that is then deleted in test_delete_screening. Frontend e2e created and left one screening for admin (and then could not delete from History page due to stale-cache bug — not blocking).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_anemia_backend.py is reusable as regression. Gemini call takes ~5-8s per image so screening with both images returns in <15s. Toast component overlaps nav for ~3s after login/analysis-complete; if writing playwright, either dismiss the toast (close button) or wait 4s before clicking nav. Frontend stores JWT in localStorage AND backend sets access_token httpOnly cookie. CORS uses wildcard + no-credentials, so prefer Bearer header.
rca of the issue: History page stale-cache bug: History.js likely fetches on initial mount but doesn't re-fetch when the user returns to it after creating a new screening (no dependency on location/navigation). Quick fix: useEffect(() => { fetchScreenings(); }, [location.pathname]) or invalidate query in Screen.js after successful POST /api/screenings before navigating to /results.
